generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuationFE : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Converts desired speed commands into motor voltage commands for controlling the vehicle's speed via the Vedder Electronic Speed Controller"
]

instance SteeringServoActuationFE : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Manages steering angle adjustments using onboard servo actuators"
]

instance ACCControllingFE : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Computes desired speed based on lead vehicle distance and sends actuation commands back to the physical twin for implementation by motor and steering systems (ISO 15622 Performance Class I compliance)"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides 3D point cloud data for object detection and tracking in the vehicle's proximity (Hokuyo UST-10LX model)"
]

instance IntelDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Captures real-time depth information for environmental perception (Intel Deepsense D435i model)"
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects real-time data on ego vehicle speed, steering angle, and other critical parameters"
]

// C4: Data Transmission from Sensing Components
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoLiDAR
]

instance DepthImageData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDepthCamera
]

instance EgoVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

// C5: Data Processing Models
instance DBSCANClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Processes LiDAR point clouds for object track clustering via DBSCAN algorithm"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Enabler
]

instance KalmanFilterModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Implements Kalman filtering for object tracking from processed point cloud data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Enabler
]

instance ACCControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Implements adaptive cruise control logic (ISO 15622 Performance Class I compliance) for speed adjustments based on lead vehicle detection"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Enabler
]

// C6: Data Storage
instance HistoricalVehicleStateDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "SQL database storing historical ego vehicle state data externally to the vehicle for analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EgoVehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SQLDatabaseEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "SQL database storing processed object track data from LiDAR clustering and Kalman filtering"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SQLDatabaseEnabler
]

instance ProcessedObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks generated from raw LiDAR data via DBSCAN clustering and Kalman filtering"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Enabler
]

// C7: Visualization Models
instance CADVehicleRepresentation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D CAD-derived representation of the vehicle for real-time rendering in the digital twin visualization"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Enabler
]

instance EnvironmentRepresentation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D CAD-derived representation of the vehicle's environment for real-time rendering"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Enabler
]

// C10: Enablers
instance ROS2Enabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot Operating System 2 middleware for inter-component communication and modular software development"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataProcessingService, VisualizationService, ControlService
]

instance SQLDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database infrastructure for storing and managing historical vehicle state and object track data"
    DTDFVocab:enables ReportGenerationService
]

instance NVIDIAJetpackEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetpack 5.0 SDK for programming NVIDIA Jetson NX/Orin Nano microcomputers"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataProcessingService
]

// C11: Services
instance DataProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Processes raw sensor data through DBSCAN clustering, Kalman filtering, and ACC control logic offloading to cloud-based compute"
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectTrackAnalysisInsight, ControlCommandGenerationAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of ego vehicle speed, steering angle, detected object tracks, and projected driving path via 3D visualization interface"
    DTDFVocab:provides DrivingPathVisualizationInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC control logic from vehicle ECUs to digital twin entity for real-time processing and enables remote management of ACC parameters"
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteACCManagementAction
]

instance ReportGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generates human-readable reports of object tracks and vehicle state as time-series data for developers and operators"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalAnalysisReport
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ObjectTrackAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Data-driven insights from historical vehicle state and object track reports informing ACC tuning and driver behavior analysis"
]

instance DrivingPathVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time 3D visualization of detected objects, ego vehicle position, and projected driving path for driver monitoring"
]

instance ControlCommandGenerationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic generation of speed adjustment commands based on lead vehicle detection and ACC control logic"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RemoteACCManagementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote enabling/disabling of ACC, setting desired speeds, and triggering emergency braking commands via Control Panel UI"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance HistoricalAnalysisReport : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generation of time-series reports of vehicle state and object tracks for performance optimization and predictive maintenance analysis"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C12: Environment
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HokuyoLiDAR, IntelDepthCamera, VehicleStateSensor,
              MotorActuationFE, SteeringServoActuationFE, ACCControllingFE,
              DBSCANClusteringModel, KalmanFilterModel, ACCControlLogicModel,
              CADVehicleRepresentation, EnvironmentRepresentation
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The virtual-to-physical interaction includes:
  - **Actuation Commands**: The DT’s ACC Controlling FE computes desired speed and sends commands to the Physical Twin Entity’s Actuation FE, which translates these into motor voltage commands for the Vedder Speed Controller and steering servo adjustments.
  - **Remote Management Commands**: User commands (enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, emergency braking) are transmitted via the User Interface FE to the Physical Twin Entity for immediate execution.
  - **Data Storage Requests**: The DT’s Data Storage component (external to the vehicle) receives and stores ego vehicle state and object track data for historical analysis."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates on a **real-time time scale** with strict synchronization requirements:
  - **Data Collection and Processing**: Object tracks and ego vehicle state are updated at a rate aligned with sensor refresh cycles (e.g., LiDAR and camera frames).
  - **End-to-End Latency**: Must maintain ≤ **100 ms** latency from the vehicle to the DT Entity for real-time control (per 3GPP TS 22.185).
  - **Clock Synchronization**: All components must share a clock with **100 ms accuracy** for event ordering and data integrity."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is organized into three primary entities (ISO 23247 framework), with custom adaptations:
  - **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts sensor data processing (LiDAR clustering, Kalman filtering) and actuation components (motor/steering control).
  - **Digital Twin Entity**: Implements core DT logic (ACC control, data collection, reporting) and is split into sub-FEs (e.g., Data Collection FE, Controlling FE).
  - **User Entity**: Consolidated into two subcomponents (3D Visualization FE and Control Panel FE) for user interaction.
  - **Cross-System Entity**: Data Translation FE ensures inter-entity communication via ROS2 (internal) and Wi-Fi (external) protocols."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT spans the following lifecycle stages:
  - **Design Phase**: Used for cyber-physical experimentation and simulation of ACC behavior (e.g., 3D CAD integration for virtual prototyping).
  - **Operational Phase**: Enables real-time monitoring, remote management, and predictive maintenance during vehicle operation.
  - **Representation Phase**: Operates as a **real-time operational twin** (as-operated) rather than an idealized or manufactured twin."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Verification**: Real-time latency testing (≤100 ms) and clock synchronization validation (±100 ms).
  - **Validation**: ACC control logic validated against ISO 15622 standards for Performance Class I systems.
  - **Uncertainty Handling**: LiDAR and camera data processed via DBSCAN clustering and Kalman filtering to mitigate noise in object detection.
  - **Error Mitigation**: Data Translation FE handles unit conversions and message alignment to prevent communication failures."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT technical connection employs:
  - **Internal Communication**: ROS2 network for inter-component communication within the vehicle.
  - **External Communication**: Wi-Fi network for inter-entity communication between the vehicle and DT Entity.
  - **Protocols**: Data Translation FE serializes and aligns message types for compatibility across entities.
  - **Latency Constraints**: End-to-end latency ≤100 ms (3GPP TS 22.185) for real-time control."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is deployed as follows:
  - **Digital Twin Entity**: Hosted on external cloud infrastructure (not specified in documents) for compute offloading.
  - **Physical Twin Entity**: Deployed on-board the 1/10th-scale autonomous vehicle using NVIDIA Jetson NX/Orin Nano microcomputers.
  - **User Interface**: Deployed as a web-based or ROS2-based application accessible via Wi-Fi from remote locations."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration involves:
  - **External Systems**: Data exchange with other automotive DTs or IoT platforms for fleet coordination or remote diagnostics (e.g., cloud-based fleet management systems).
  - **Simulation Integration**: Coupling with 3D CAD tools (e.g., for virtual prototyping) and AI simulation frameworks (e.g., for training AI agents via DT data)."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership and privacy are managed as follows:
  - **Data Storage**: Ego vehicle state and object track data are stored externally to the vehicle (e.g., SQL databases) and owned by the DT service provider.
  - **Access Control**: User permissions restrict remote management commands to authorized operators/developers.
  - **Ethical Considerations**: No explicit mention of anonymization or GDPR compliance, but data is treated as proprietary for the autonomous vehicle’s development."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following standards:
  - **Architectural Standard**: ISO 23247 (Digital Twin Manufacturing Framework) for entity-based reference architecture (RA) adaptation.
  - **Automotive Standards**: ISO 15622 for ACC control logic (Performance Class I).
  - **Communication Standard**: ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2) for inter-component communication.
  - **Clock Synchronization**: No stricter standard than ±100 ms (due to hardware limitations)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
  - **Remote Operation**: DT enables remote management of ACC but requires strict access controls to prevent unauthorized commands (e.g., emergency braking).
  - **Safety-Critical Requirements**: End-to-end latency ≤100 ms ensures real-time control without risking accidents.
  - **Data Integrity**: Clock synchronization (±100 ms) and Data Translation FE prevent timing/format inconsistencies.
  - **Human-in-the-Loop**: DT falls under the 'Fully Autonomous Digital Twin with Human-in-the-Loop' taxonomy (David and Bork), ensuring oversight for critical operations."
]
